dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 30/50, Ta'leef Collective shows medium donor fit by addressing the urgent religious and cultural needs of the Muslim community [1]. It offers various giving methods, including zakat-eligible donations, to facilitate community support [1][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization's impact score of 30/50 reflects its focus on capacity building through programs like Convert Care, though its program expense ratio is currently 22.1% [2][3]. While it serves a vital community role, the cost per beneficiary is estimated at 2170.104, and data confidence remains low at 0.3 [2].

baseline_narrative.summary: Based in Fremont, California, Ta'leef Collective offers specialized programming including Convert Care and the Inheritors program to assist newcomers and lifelong Muslims in their spiritual journeys [2][3]. The organization reported a total revenue of $1,397,233 and maintains a zakat-eligible status to support its religious and educational mission [1][3].

strengths.detail: The organization demonstrates exceptional lean fundraising, spending only <cite id="11">$0.02 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.
With a history <cite id="12">dating back to 2005</cite>, the organization has over 19 years of experience in the religious and cultural sector.
Ta'leef successfully managed <cite id="13">395 initial community contacts</cite> for new Muslims and distributed significant direct aid.

strengths_deep_dive: Ta'leef Collective distinguishes itself through its highly specialized 'Convert Care' and 'Inheritors' programs, which address a critical gap in the American Muslim landscape. Their ability to facilitate <cite id="9">101 shahadas</cite> in a single year demonstrates a high level of engagement with seekers. Furthermore, the organization maintains strong governance standards with a <cite id="6">10-member board that is 100% independent</cite>, ensuring oversight is not compromised by internal conflicts of interest.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2005, Ta'leef Collective is a California-based nonprofit dedicated to providing a <cite id="1">dedicated space and community support</cite> for individuals seeking a healthy realization of Islam. The organization specializes in <cite id="2">Convert Care and the Inheritors program</cite>, which are designed to assist both newcomers and lifelong Muslims in their spiritual journeys. In 2023, the organization reported a <cite id="3">total revenue of $1,397,233</cite>, though it has faced significant financial volatility with a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of -44.6%</cite>. Ta'leef operates as a <cite id="5">zakat-eligible entity</cite>, specifically directing funds toward categories such as the poor (fuqara), the needy (masakin), and those whose hearts are to be reconciled (muallaf). While the organization maintains a <cite id="6">100% independent board</cite>, its financial efficiency is a point of concern, with a <cite id="7">program expense ratio of only 22.1%</cite> reported in recent filings. Despite these fiscal challenges, Ta'leef remains a niche provider of <cite id="8">specialized support for Muslim converts</cite>, facilitating <cite id="9">101 shahadas</cite> and providing <cite id="10">$140,000 in zakat assistance</cite> according to their 2023 impact data.
